scenario: Multi-client flag orders in single supplier thread — client separation via implicit text only
input_threads:
=== THREAD 1: Supplier Group (Ashish + Malerkotla Supplier) ===
[20:52, 3/2/2026] Ashish Chhabra: lancer flag 06 nos same as shown thick quality
[20:52, 3/2/2026] Ashish Chhabra: [Photo dropped — T flag sample]
[20:52, 3/2/2026] Ashish Chhabra: T flag sirf dena hai 04 nos same mota cloth
[20:53, 3/2/2026] Ashish Chhabra: [Photo dropped — client-specific flag sample]
[20:53, 3/2/2026] Ashish Chhabra: 01 each 1+1+1 flags blazer cloth pe 3x2 feet ke
[20:53, 3/2/2026] Ashish Chhabra: jaldi karwa do
[12:45, 3/5/2026] Malerkotla Supplier: Size....
[12:47, 3/5/2026] Ashish Chhabra: 6x4 feet 05 flags all 01 nos each jaisa pic hai aapka hi bana hua hai blazer kapde pe badhiya bana ke dena hai
[16:33, 3/9/2026] Ashish Chhabra: [5 photos dropped — flag samples per client]
[16:33, 3/9/2026] Ashish Chhabra: yeh 01 client ka hai sir
[16:34, 3/9/2026] Ashish Chhabra: [Photo dropped]
[16:34, 3/9/2026] Ashish Chhabra: yeh 1 client ka hai
[16:34, 3/9/2026] Ashish Chhabra: baki T flag lancer flag 3x2 wale flag sab 1 bande ka hai
expected_output: At least 3 separate flag order parent tasks: (1) Lancer flags — 6 nos, thick quality — Client C (identity in dropped photos). (2) T flags — 4 nos, thick cloth — Client B (identity in dropped photos). (3) Client-specific blazer cloth flags — 3x2 ft — Client A (5 photos dropped, 3/9). All urgent. Client identities unresolvable from text alone — agent must flag for human review.
challenge: Client separation cues are entirely in missing photos. Only implicit text signals: "yeh 01 client ka hai", "yeh 1 client ka hai", "baki... sab 1 bande ka hai". No explicit client names anywhere in thread.
pass_criteria: PASS: 3+ separate flag order tasks created, each flagged as ambiguous (client identity in missing photos). FAIL: All flags merged into one order or fewer than 3 tasks created without flagging missing client identity.
notes: Real data — Malerkotla supplier thread (incomplete). New challenge not in synthetic cases: client separation entirely dependent on dropped images. Key question for Ashish validation: are implicit text cues like "baki sab 1 bande ka hai" typical in real chats?

scenario: Payment confirmed — delivery shortfall and quality rejection raised 2 days later in same thread
input_threads:
=== THREAD 1: Supplier Group (Ashish + Malerkotla Supplier) ===
[16:41, 3/16/2026] Mrs Samita Roy Staff Enquiry: ok sir thank u
[16:41, 3/16/2026] [Supplier sent QR code for payment — image dropped]
[16:41, 3/16/2026] [Ashish sent payment confirmation screenshot — image dropped]
[17:14, 3/16/2026] Ashish Chhabra: paid 28000 all cleared boss
[17:14, 3/16/2026] Ashish Chhabra: thanks a lot for all the help
[18:47, 3/18/2026] Ashish Chhabra: sir car flags 05 nahi mile
[18:47, 3/18/2026] Ashish Chhabra: wo kahan hai
[18:47, 3/18/2026] Ashish Chhabra: and india flag and hathi wala dono jahan white hai bahut ganda hai
[18:48, 3/18/2026] Ashish Chhabra: reject ho sakta hai
expected_output: Two post-delivery follow-up tasks: (1) Locate and deliver missing car flags — 5 nos — from Malerkotla supplier (urgent). (2) Quality review: India flag and elephant flag — white quality defect — client rejection risk. Order status: Blocked/In Progress — NOT complete despite payment. 28000 paid but delivery incomplete and quality disputed.
challenge: Payment confirmation ("paid 28000 all cleared") on 3/16 is a strong order-closure signal. Quality defect and delivery shortfall raised 2 days later in same thread. Agent must not treat payment as order closure.
pass_criteria: PASS: Agent creates 2 post-delivery follow-up tasks (missing items + quality issue) and marks order Blocked or In Progress despite payment. FAIL: Order marked Complete after payment; no follow-up tasks for missing car flags or quality rejection.
notes: Real data — Malerkotla supplier thread (incomplete). New risk R6 — not in original framework. Mrs Samita Roy is Ashish's staff member. Actual resolution chain (off-platform): missing items noticed on collection day → Ashish verbally asked Mrs Samita Roy to update supplier on WhatsApp → she did not → 2 days later no update, she had forgotten and claimed she couldn't reach supplier by phone → supplier called urgently, notified verbally → still no WhatsApp group update → supplier eventually shared courier consignment note image on chat confirming dispatch. Verbal instruction gap (R7a variant) is a key contributing failure. Courier note is an image — agent needs image support to extract dispatch confirmation.

scenario: Single-thread delivery completion with supplier QC pass and dispatch
input_threads:
=== THREAD 1: Supplier Group (Ashish + Staff 1 + Supplier) ===
[24 Mar 09:00] Staff 1: Sharma ji maal kab tak aayega?
[24 Mar 09:15] Supplier: Kal subah tak aa jayega, 50 bags atta
[24 Mar 10:00] Staff 1: Ok sir confirmed
[25 Mar 09:30] Staff 1: Maal aa gaya sir, sab check kar liya theek hai
[25 Mar 10:00] Ashish: Good, dispatch karo Sata Bty Bn ko aaj hi
[25 Mar 11:00] Staff 1: Vehicle ready hai, challan bana rahe hain
[25 Mar 14:00] Staff 1: Dispatch ho gaya sir, vehicle number AS01 BK 4521
[25 Mar 18:00] Staff 1: Delivery ho gayi sir, Sub Arvind ne receive kar liya
expected_output: Single parent task: Atta delivery to Sata Bty Bn. Full lifecycle: supplier delivery → QC pass → dispatch → delivery confirmed. All milestone nodes completed. Implicit tasks: delivery challan, gate pass, GRN, invoice, payment follow-up (30d).

notes: Synthetic baseline for R1-D. Single thread, straightforward lifecycle.

scenario: Conflicting delivery signals across three threads with ambiguous entity
input_threads:
=== THREAD 1: Client Group (Ashish + Staff 1 + Client) ===
[24 Mar 09:00] Client: Sir maal kab tak milega?
[24 Mar 09:15] Ashish: Kal tak ho jayega sir
[24 Mar 15:00] Client: Agency ne bola abhi tak nahi aaya

=== THREAD 2: Supplier Group (Ashish + Staff 1 + Supplier) ===
[24 Mar 10:00] Staff 1: Kapoor ji maal ready hai?
[24 Mar 10:15] Supplier: Haan bhai dispatch ho gaya subah
[24 Mar 16:00] Supplier: Aapka driver aaya nahi abhi tak

=== THREAD 3: All Staff Group ===
[24 Mar 11:00] Staff 1: Maal ready hai godown mein
[24 Mar 14:00] Haque Babu: Sir kiska maal hai ye? Do order pending hain
[24 Mar 14:30] Ashish: Confirm karo Kapoor wala hai ya Sharma wala
[24 Mar 16:30] Staff 1: Sir driver bol raha hai traffic mein fasa hai
expected_output: Multiple ambiguity flags: (1) 'Agency' in Thread 1 — vague entity, which client? (2) Conflicting dispatch status — supplier says dispatched but driver hasn't arrived. (3) 'Maal ready hai godown mein' — unclear which order (Kapoor or Sharma). (4) Two pending orders — entity ambiguity on which order the goods belong to.
challenge: Three threads with conflicting signals: supplier says dispatched, staff says ready at godown, client says not received. Plus entity ambiguity — 'Agency' is vague, and two pending orders create confusion about which maal is being discussed.
pass_criteria: PASS: At least 3 distinct ambiguity flags raised (entity, status conflict, order attribution). Next steps recommend resolving ambiguity before action. FAIL: Agent silently resolves ambiguities or misses the dispatch status conflict.
notes: Hardest ambiguity scenario — cross-thread conflicting signals plus entity ambiguity.

scenario: Delivery completed across two threads but invoice and payment follow-up missing
input_threads:
=== THREAD 1: Client Group (Ashish + Staff 1 + Client) ===
[20 Mar 09:00] Client: Sir items mil gaye, thank you
[20 Mar 09:15] Ashish: Great sir, glad to hear
[20 Mar 09:16] Ashish: @Staff 1 invoice raise karo aaj hi
[24 Mar 10:00] Client: Sir invoice abhi tak nahi aaya

=== THREAD 2: All Staff Group ===
[20 Mar 10:00] Staff 1: Client ne confirm kar diya delivery ho gayi
[20 Mar 10:15] Ashish: Toh invoice banao aur challan ka copy bhi attach karo
[20 Mar 15:00] Mousumi Banik: Sir challan number kya hai?
[20 Mar 15:30] Staff 1: 4521
[24 Mar 11:00] Ashish: Invoice raise hua ya nahi? Client bol raha hai nahi mila
expected_output: Parent task: Delivery to client — completed but invoice pending. Implicit tasks: (1) Invoice raising — overdue (4 days since delivery, client has complained). (2) Delivery challan — challan 4521 referenced but formal document not confirmed sent to client. (3) Payment follow-up — cannot start until invoice raised. (4) GST reconciliation for this invoice.
challenge: Delivery is confirmed across two threads but the post-delivery cadence tasks (invoice, challan, payment follow-up) are not completed. Agent must detect these as overdue implicit tasks, not just note them.
pass_criteria: PASS: Invoice flagged as overdue with urgency (client complained). Challan, payment follow-up, and GST reconciliation surfaced as implicit tasks. FAIL: Only delivery marked complete with no post-delivery tasks.
notes: Tests cadence detection across two threads — delivery confirmed in both but administrative follow-through is missing.
